LJCBackupProfiles.summary: Represents a collection of backup profiles.

remarks.para:
The profiles text file can contain a definition for one or more backup profiles.
Each profile contains the values needed for a specific backup operation.

A backup profile starts with the "Backup" command followed by a comma and the backup name, then followed by the profile values.
The sample backup name contains the target backup drive and target codeline name.

Backup, CLJCProjects
SourceRoot, C:\Users\User\Documents\Visual Studio 2022
SourceCodeline, LJCProjectsDev
TargetRoot, C:\Users\User\Documents\Visual Studio 2022
TargetCodeline, LJCProjects
ChangesFilespec, LJCProjects\ChangesLJCProjects.txt
SkipFoldersFilespec, LJCProjects\SkipFoldersLJCProjects.txt
IncludeFilters, *.cs|*.sln|*.csproj|*.Designer.cs|*.resx|*.config
IncludeFilters, Doc\*.xml|*.cmd|*.txt|*.sql
SkipFiles, ChangesFile.txt|BackupLog.txt|MissingFolders.txt SkipFiles,?Build*.cmd|ClearBuild.cmd|UpdateAll.cmd

The IncludeFilters and SkipFiles values can contain multiple entries.
Values with multiple entries are combined into a single list when read by the program.

ConstructorParams.summary: Initializes an object instance with the supplied values.

remarks.para: The parameterized constructor sets the Filespec property with the filespec parameter.
The Filespec property loads the backup profiles from the profiles file.
Loads the backup profiles from the profiles file into the collection.

Clone.summary: Creates and returns a clone of the object.

LJCHasItems.summary: Checks if the collection has items.
LJCHasItems.returns: true if the collection has items; otherwise false.

Filespec.summary: Gets or sets the content filespec.

remarks.para: When the backup profiles Filespec property is set, it looks for the profiles file with the suppied name.
If the profiles file does not exist, it calls the CreateSampleProfilesFile() method to create a sample profiles file.
The profiles file is then loaded into the collection with the LoadProfiles() method.

NameIndexer.summary: Retrieve the collection element by name.
